title: Harden company/F10 content rendering to avoid unsafe HTML and links
description:
Source: tech-scan
Category: security
Priority: P0

## Problem Description
Company F10 content is converted into HTML and rendered with dangerouslySetInnerHTML. Current escaping is insufficient for attribute contexts and link rendering, creating XSS and unsafe-link risk from upstream content.

## Impact
Critical impact on frontend safety because externally sourced company content is displayed directly in the main stock-detail experience.

## Suggestion
Replace raw HTML injection with React-rendered structured content or sanitize rigorously, escape attribute quotes, validate protocols, and add rel="noopener noreferrer" to links.

## Evidence
web/src/pages/stock/StockDetail.tsx:1025
web/src/lib/tdx-parser.ts:74
web/src/lib/tdx-parser.ts:132
web/src/lib/tdx-parser.ts:144

## Confidence

title: Add transparent status, progress, and failure reporting for batch screening
description:
Source: tech-scan|product-walkthrough
Category: ux
Priority: P0

## Problem Description
The screening flow currently hides per-code failures and skipped items. The server returns only successful rows while silently continuing on fetch/no-data errors, and the UI shows generic loading/error states. Users need per-stock status, aggregate success/failed counts, and retryable failures to trust screening outcomes.

## Impact
High impact on screening reliability, user trust, and debugging of missing results across watchlists and block scans.

## Suggestion
Extend /api/screen to return per-code status and error reasons, expose progress and failed/skipped counts in the UI, and allow retrying failed codes without rerunning the entire batch.

title: Add request bounds and performance controls to screening and stock-compare APIs
description:
Source: tech-scan|product-walkthrough
Category: performance
Priority: P1

## Problem Description
Screening accepts arbitrary code lists and computes sequentially per code, while stock comparison fans out into many quote requests per block member. Both flows can become slow, resource-heavy, and fragile under larger inputs.

## Impact
High impact on responsiveness and server stability for popular workflows like watchlist scans and block-relative comparison.

## Suggestion
Validate and deduplicate codes, cap batch sizes, add bounded concurrency and caching, batch quote retrieval where possible, and paginate or limit heavy comparison details.

title: Make indicator configuration safer and more explainable
description:
Source: product-walkthrough
Category: usability
Priority: P1

## Problem Description
The settings page writes directly to live indicator config and offers a hardcoded sample reset without dirty-state protection, preview, backup, or impact visibility. Users can accidentally overwrite production parameters and change downstream analytics unexpectedly.

## Impact
High impact because configuration changes affect stock detail charts and screening logic across the product.

## Suggestion
Add unsaved-change warnings, confirm/reset safeguards, backup/export/import, validation summaries, and stock-level preview of resolved parameters before saving.

title: Expose watchlist groups and notes in the Web UI
description:
Source: product-walkthrough
Category: feature-gap
Priority: P1

## Problem Description
Watchlist storage and APIs already support groups and notes, but the main Watchlist page does not expose them. Users cannot organize stocks by strategy, sector, or conviction directly in the product.

## Impact
High impact for users managing larger watchlists, reducing the product’s usefulness as a working research workspace.

## Suggestion
Add grouped views, group filters, inline group/note editing, and preserve group/note metadata when adding from search or screen results.

title: Surface data freshness and sync diagnostics across watchlist, screen, and stock detail
description:
Source: tech-scan|product-walkthrough
Category: feature-gap
Priority: P1

## Problem Description
Daily K-line sync exists, but users cannot see per-stock freshness, last-sync timestamps, partial failures, or stale-data indicators in core analysis flows. Recent fixes for corrupt/legacy K-line data indicate ongoing data-quality risk that should be visible in the product.

## Impact
High impact on decision confidence because users cannot tell whether indicators and charts are current, partially synced, or recovered from invalid source data.

## Suggestion
Add sync-status panels, last-sync/freshness badges, partial-failure details, and visible data-quality diagnostics such as skipped invalid rows or fallback data usage.

title: Increase maintainability and test coverage around large core modules
description:
Source: tech-scan|product-walkthrough
Category: maintainability
Priority: P2

## Problem Description
Core product behavior is concentrated in very large server, CLI, screen, and stock-detail modules, while automated coverage remains sparse for server routes and frontend workflows. This raises regression risk and slows new feature delivery.

## Impact
Medium-to-high impact on delivery speed and confidence when extending product workflows such as alerts, richer screening, and advanced watchlist features.

## Suggestion
Split handlers/pages by domain responsibility and add focused handler/component regression tests for stock detail, screen, settings, and error-path behavior.

title: Improve mobile and narrow-screen compatibility
description:
Source: product-walkthrough
Category: compatibility
Priority: P2

## Problem Description
The web UI is desktop-first: fixed sider/header search patterns, wide tables, and rigid multi-column layouts make screening, settings, and stock detail hard to use on phones or narrow screens.

## Impact
Medium-to-high impact for mobile or tablet usage, especially for users checking data quickly outside a desktop environment.

## Suggestion
Add responsive breakpoints, auto-collapse or drawer navigation, stacked filter layouts, full-width search, and mobile-safe table/chart layouts.
